description: Audio zone definitions for all 4 Valley of Survival quests. Each zone has ambient loop metadata, voice asset URLs, attenuation radius, and speaker label colors matching Agent #15 dialogue system notes.

Narrator_WaterHole.text: The water hole is close. Listen — insects, frogs, the sound of life. Where there is water, there is danger. Move carefully.
Narrator_WaterHole.speaker: Narrator

Elder_RaptorWarning.text: The raptors hunt in packs. If you see one, there are three more you cannot see. Your only advantage is fire. Use it.
Elder_RaptorWarning.speaker: Elder

ambient_loop.description: Insects, frogs, slow water movement, distant bird calls
ambient_loop.ue5_implementation: MetaSound asset: MS_WaterHole_Ambient — layered: insects (high), frogs (mid), water trickle (low), random bird stab every 8-15s

danger_sting.description: Sudden insect silence — player cue that danger is near

ambient_loop.description: Open plains wind, distant herd rumble (sub-bass 40-80Hz), occasional bird scatter
ambient_loop.ue5_implementation: MetaSound asset: MS_Migration_Ambient — layered: wind (continuous), sub-bass herd rumble (looping), bird scatter (random stab 20-40s)

danger_sting.description: Sub-bass builds to full thunder as herd approaches

ambient_loop.description: Near-silence — sparse wind, no insects (they fled), occasional twig snap, distant raptor vocalization
ambient_loop.ue5_implementation: MetaSound asset: MS_RaptorClearing_Ambient — minimal: wind (very low), random twig snap (every 30-60s), raptor distant call (every 45-90s)
ambient_loop.design_note: The silence IS the sound design here. Absence of insects = danger signal. Walter Murch principle: what is NOT there is more powerful.

danger_sting.description: Single low heartbeat, then absolute silence before raptor attack

ambient_loop.description: Deep sub-bass ground vibration (footsteps), bird scatter in canopy, low Brachio vocalizations (infrasound-like 20-40Hz), distant tree cracking
ambient_loop.ue5_implementation: MetaSound asset: MS_BrachioHerd_Ambient — layered: sub-bass pulse (synced to Brachio step cycle ~3s), bird scatter (random), low vocal rumble (every 20-30s), tree crack (every 60-90s)
ambient_loop.design_note: Player should FEEL the Brachiosaurus before they see it. Sub-bass at 30-40Hz creates physical sensation of scale.

danger_sting.description: All birds scatter simultaneously + ground rumble intensifies — herd is moving

global_audio_design.music_system: Adaptive — no music during exploration (ambient only). Music enters only on quest completion or combat.
global_audio_design.combat_music: Percussion-only: bone drums, stone percussion, no melodic instruments. Tempo increases with danger proximity.
global_audio_design.quest_completion_sting: 3-note motif: low bone drum + mid wood percussion + high stone tap. Duration 2.5s. Plays once on quest complete.

day_night_audio_shift.dawn: Bird calls increase, insect fade, wind rises
day_night_audio_shift.midday: Peak insect volume, heat shimmer sound (high freq hiss), reduced bird activity
day_night_audio_shift.dusk: Insect transition (day to night species), predator activity sounds increase
day_night_audio_shift.night: Night insects, owl calls, distant predator vocalizations, fire crackle (if player has fire)

global_audio_design.walter_murch_principle: The sound that does not exist is often more powerful than the sound that does. Silence = danger. Sudden silence = immediate threat.
global_audio_design.rdr2_principle: Music the player never consciously notices but that changes what they feel is the peak of interactive composition.

freesound_catalog.name: crickets birds heathland ambient
freesound_catalog.usage: Primary ambient loop — insects + birds layer
